content:
I'm not aware of any "recent issue of Notification Sound Alerts (NSA)" and I find no reference to it on the net. I did find that a few versions back, Thunderbird on Windows stopped liking the use of MP3 filed for the notification sound. I simply made a new version of my custom sound in WAV format.

Have you tried just changing to a different sound?

Based on your wording above, I predict you will dismiss this suggestion.
creator: rednoise

content:
Thanks so much for taking the time to reply. Much appreciated

Funny you should suggest a .wav file as a solution, cause the Alert is currently set as precisely that. I've been experimenting with different sound files, hoping one of then would kick in, but to no avail.

A quick search on Google for TB not sending sound alerts for incoming mail brings up literally pages of suggestions, dating as you mentioned from back in 2014 right on through Ap. 1 of this year. There's one posted in the forum right now under the title " 'Not getting a sound when emails arrive in Thunderbird' Support Mozilla - https://support.mozilla.org › questions - Apr 1, 2026 — I've checked all the notification settings and they are on, I've check focus assist and it is off. Any ideas, I've run out of mine."

Thanks again.
creator: balducci1

content: I am wondering... will you do better if you disable push notifications in account settings. (allow immediate notifications when a new messages arrive). That is a different way of getting new mail (pushed out rather that getting mail) and it might at least identify a bug if getting mail that way turns out to be the issue.
creator: MattAuSupport

content:
Thanks for taking the time to reply. Disable it...hummm. That's almost contrary to what we're trying to accomplish, tho, isn't it? Don't we WANT notifications when new messages hit the Inbox?

What's your logic on going this route. It's not yet clear to me. I know you said "That is a different way of getting new mail (pushed out rather that getting mail)". But if we're telling the system NOT to allow notifications, then how are new messages going to sound a notification? We just told the system NOT to alert us.

Can you help me follow your logic on this? And to be honest, this is the first new and original fix I've ever heard, so well done on thinking this through....even thought I don't understand it yet!! haha
creator: balducci1

content:
Is it a bug? Is it a bug in Thunderbird?

If you are using a gmail imap account then Google do not consider it a bug and as it is their IMAP implementation that has the quirk there is nothing to do but live with it..
